name: Inter-American Convention on Human Rights (Pact of San Jose / Pacto de San José de Costa Rica 1969)
summary: The Inter-American Convention on Human Rights (popularly the Pact of San Jose / Pacto de San José de Costa Rica) is a Latin American supranational meta-framework human-rights binding-treaty instrument adopted by the Organization of American States (OAS) — structurally distinctive globally as one of three modern regional binding-treaty frameworks operating both formal human-rights binding-treaty framework + dedicated judicial-body framework (American Convention on Human Rights adopted 22 November 1969 + effective 18 July 1978 — formal Inter-American regional binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive human-rights framework + Inter-American Court of Human Rights IACtHR formal judicial-body framework, structurally distinctive globally as one of three modern regional human-rights frameworks with formal regional-court framework alongside ECHR + ACHPR-protocol-establishing-African-Court, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-human-rights frameworks which operate without formal regional-court framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Inter-American Article 17 family-rights framework (American Convention Article 17 framework on Rights of the Family — formal foundational family-rights framework underlying Inter-American parental-rights + child-protection case-law framework, distinct from ECHR Article 8 right to respect for private and family life framework operating through individual-rights-frame, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional human-rights binding-treaty framework operating formal family-rights-as-direct-right framework), as the central jurisdiction of the most distinctive modern Inter-American Court of Human Rights case-law framework on family-rights + child-rights (IACtHR Advisory Opinion OC-17/02 of 28 August 2002 on Juridical Condition and Human Rights of the Child + Atala Riffo and Daughters v. Chile 24 February 2012 + Fornerón and Daughter v. Argentina 27 April 2012 + multiple subsequent IACtHR cases applying Article 17 + 19 + 24 frameworks — formal IACtHR family-rights + child-rights case-law framework, structurally distinctive globally as the most-developed modern regional human-rights framework on family-rights + child-rights), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal Inter-American American Convention Article 19 children's-rights framework (American Convention Article 19 framework on Rights of the Child — formal Inter-American framework predating UNCRC 1989 by 20 years, structurally distinctive globally as the earliest formal regional-binding-treaty children's-rights framework). The Inter-American Convention on Human Rights is binding upon 23 of 35 OAS member states that have ratified the Convention as of 2026 (excluding United States + Canada + most-of-Caribbean-CARICOM frameworks). The Convention is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal Article 17 family-rights + Article 19 children's-rights frameworks operate substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework. The Inter-American Court of Human Rights is the apex judicial body framework; the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights is the formal monitoring body framework. The Inter-American Convention on Human Rights is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: American Convention on Human Rights 1969 (Pact of San Jose)
statutory_framework.title: American Convention on Human Rights
statutory_framework.relevance: American Convention on Human Rights signed 22 November 1969 in San José, Costa Rica + effective 18 July 1978 — formal Inter-American regional binding-treaty framework establishing comprehensive human-rights framework.

statutory_framework.citation: American Convention Article 17 (Rights of the Family)
statutory_framework.title: American Convention Article 17
statutory_framework.relevance: American Convention Article 17 framework on Rights of the Family — formal foundational family-rights framework underlying Inter-American parental-rights + child-protection case-law framework.

statutory_framework.citation: American Convention Article 19 (Rights of the Child)
statutory_framework.title: American Convention Article 19
statutory_framework.relevance: American Convention Article 19 framework on Rights of the Child — formal Inter-American framework predating UNCRC 1989 by 20 years.

statutory_framework.citation: IACtHR Advisory Opinion OC-17/02 2002 + Atala Riffo 2012 + Fornerón 2012
statutory_framework.title: IACtHR Family + Child Rights Case-Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Inter-American Court of Human Rights Advisory Opinion OC-17/02 of 28 August 2002 on Juridical Condition and Human Rights of the Child + Atala Riffo and Daughters v. Chile 24 February 2012 + Fornerón and Daughter v. Argentina 27 April 2012 — formal IACtHR family-rights + child-rights case-law framework.

statutory_framework.citation: OAS Charter 1948 + Inter-American Commission on Human Rights 1959
statutory_framework.title: OAS Charter + Inter-American Commission
statutory_framework.relevance: Organization of American States Charter signed 30 April 1948 + Inter-American Commission on Human Rights established 1959 — formal OAS regional supranational framework underlying American Convention on Human Rights.

apex_courts.name: Inter-American Court of Human Rights (IACtHR)
apex_courts.role: Apex judicial body framework for American Convention on Human Rights.

apex_courts.name: Inter-American Commission on Human Rights
apex_courts.role: Inter-American Commission on Human Rights — formal Inter-American human rights monitoring body.

professional_regulators.name: OAS Secretariat
professional_regulators.role: American Convention on Human Rights implementation framework.

anonymisation_convention: American Convention on Human Rights-related individual communications are anonymised per IACtHR practice using initials.

key_developments.title: OAS Charter + American Declaration
key_developments.description: Organization of American States Charter signed 30 April 1948 + American Declaration of the Rights and Duties of Man adopted 2 May 1948 — beginning of formal OAS regional supranational framework.

key_developments.title: Inter-American Commission on Human Rights established
key_developments.description: Inter-American Commission on Human Rights established 1959 by OAS — formal Inter-American human rights monitoring body.

key_developments.title: American Convention on Human Rights adopted
key_developments.description: American Convention on Human Rights signed 22 November 1969 in San José, Costa Rica — formal Inter-American regional binding-treaty framework.

key_developments.title: American Convention enters force
key_developments.description: American Convention on Human Rights entered force 18 July 1978 — formal Inter-American regional binding-treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: IACtHR established
key_developments.description: Inter-American Court of Human Rights IACtHR established 22 May 1979 in San José, Costa Rica — formal Inter-American regional court framework.

key_developments.title: Advisory Opinion OC-17/02
key_developments.description: IACtHR Advisory Opinion OC-17/02 of 28 August 2002 on Juridical Condition and Human Rights of the Child — formal IACtHR foundational children's-rights framework.

key_developments.title: Atala Riffo + Fornerón
key_developments.description: IACtHR Atala Riffo and Daughters v. Chile 24 February 2012 + Fornerón and Daughter v. Argentina 27 April 2012 — landmark formal IACtHR family-rights + child-rights case-law framework.

structural_findings: American Convention on Human Rights operates a supranational Latin American regional human-rights binding-treaty framework — places American Convention on Human Rights in the Latin American supranational-meta-framework cluster.

One of three modern regional binding-treaty frameworks operating both formal human-rights binding-treaty framework + dedicated judicial-body framework is structurally distinctive globally — alongside ECHR + African Court Protocol frameworks.

Central jurisdiction of formal Inter-American Article 17 family-rights framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional human-rights binding-treaty framework operating formal family-rights-as-direct-right framework.

Central jurisdiction of most distinctive modern Inter-American Court of Human Rights case-law framework on family-rights + child-rights is structurally distinctive globally — Advisory Opinion OC-17/02 + Atala Riffo + Fornerón + multiple subsequent cases.

Central jurisdiction of formal Inter-American American Convention Article 19 children's-rights framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal Inter-American framework predating UNCRC 1989 by 20 years.

23 of 35 OAS member state party ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — substantial Latin American + Caribbean adoption framework but limited compared to ECHR universal European adoption framework.
Article 17 + Article 19 family-rights + children's-rights formal dual-framework is structurally distinctive globally.

1948 OAS Charter + 1959 Inter-American Commission + 1969 American Convention + 1978 entry into force + 1979 IACtHR established + 2002 Advisory Opinion OC-17/02 + 2012 Atala Riffo + Fornerón Inter-American-human-rights-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: American Convention on Human Rights jurisdiction sidecar — supranational Latin American regional human-rights binding-treaty meta-framework (American Convention on Human Rights 1969/1978 + Article 17 Rights of the Family + Article 19 Rights of the Child + IACtHR Advisory Opinion OC-17/02 2002 + Atala Riffo 2012 + Fornerón 2012 + OAS Charter 1948 + Inter-American Commission on Human Rights 1959 + IACtHR 1979). One of three modern regional binding-treaty frameworks operating both formal human-rights binding-treaty framework + dedicated judicial-body framework + central jurisdiction of formal Inter-American Article 17 family-rights framework + central jurisdiction of most distinctive modern Inter-American Court of Human Rights case-law framework on family-rights + child-rights + central jurisdiction of formal Inter-American American Convention Article 19 children's-rights framework predating UNCRC 1989 by 20 years + 23 of 35 OAS member state party ratification framework + Article 17 + Article 19 family-rights + children's-rights formal dual-framework + 1948 OAS Charter + 1959 Inter-American Commission + 1969 American Convention + 1978 entry into force + 1979 IACtHR established + 2002 Advisory Opinion OC-17/02 + 2012 Atala Riffo + Fornerón Inter-American-human-rights-framework-development sequence. OAS regional triad complete (Pact of San Jose 1969 + Belém do Pará Convention 1994 + Inter-American Convention on Return of Children 1989).
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Latin-American + supranational + regional-human-rights-binding-treaty cluster + American-Convention-Article-17-family-rights-and-Article-19-children-rights-dual-framework-globally-distinctive + Advisory-Opinion-OC-17-02-2002 + Atala-Riffo-2012 + Fornerón-2012 + IACtHR-formal-court-framework + 23-of-35-OAS-state-party-ratification + IACtHR-1979 + Inter-American-Commission-1959 + OAS-Charter-1948 + American-Declaration-1948 + ECHR-IACtHR-African-Court-three-regional-courts clusters within the corpus.
